## PiWs.route() method

Registers a WebSocket route on the underlying `uWebSockets.js` app.

**Signature:**

```typescript
route<UserData = unknown>(path: string, behavior: WebSocketRoute<UserData>['behavior']): this;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter

</th><th>

Type

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead>

path

</td><td>

string

</td><td>

WebSocket route path.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

behavior

</td><td>

[WebSocketRoute](./pi-ws.websocketroute.md)<!-- -->&lt;UserData&gt;\['behavior'\]

</td><td>

Route behavior passed to `app.ws()`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

this

The current `PiWs` instance.

## Remarks

The built-in Pi route at `${wsPrefix}/pi` is always registered separately. Use this method for any additional WebSocket endpoints your application needs.
